title: argylia viridis var. lutea
intro: Argylia viridis var. lutea, also known as the yellow leaf Argylia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Pedaliaceae and is native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-diabetic activities. Argylia viridis var. lutea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions due to its medicinal properties.
